name: Constitutional, Legal, and General Competency (100 Questions)
description: Legal, constitutional, and ethical framework for public administration in Nigeria.

subcategories.name: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles
subcategories.description: Constitutional structure, institutions, and governance principles.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the power to dismiss and exercise disciplinary control over officers on **gl 07** and above in the federal civil service is vested in?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC) is vested with the power to dismiss and exercise disciplinary control over officers in the Federal Civil Service, particularly those on GL 07 and above, as mandated by Rule 100102.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the principle that ensures government policies continue to be implemented regardless of transient changes in political leadership is known as?
questions.explanation: Permanence ensures that the civil service continues its functions despite political changes Reference: CSH Principles.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the appointments into all grades in parastatals must be based on merit, credibility, and equal opportunity with due regard to the?
questions.explanation: Rule 170302 mandates that appointments must be fair and open, ensuring merit, credibility, and equal opportunity for all with due regard to the Federal Character Principle.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who is constitutionally empowered to present the annual budget to the National Assembly for legislative approval?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Section 81 of the Constitution mandates the President to present the budget to the National Assembly.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the constitutional provision that all government funds must be centralized into a specific main account for transparency and control refers to the?
questions.explanation: The Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) is the main account into which all government revenues are paid Reference: FR General Management.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the authority responsible for the audit of government accounts and reporting findings to the national assembly is the?
questions.explanation: The Auditor-General for the Federation (AuGF) audits public accounts and reports to the National Assembly (implied by references to Audit Report and legislative oversight) Reference: AuGF Oversight.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The power of the National Assembly to make laws on matters such as currency and defense is drawn from which list in the Constitution
questions.explanation: Federal-only matters are typically found in the Exclusive Legislative List Reference: Constitutional Provisions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who is designated as the Chief Accounting Officer personally responsible for the control, custody, and disbursement of funds within a Ministry?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary or DG of an MDA serves as the Accounting Officer responsible for expenditure control, record keeping, and compliance (FR 104).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the three core values emphasized for civil servants in the civil service handbook are secrecy, efficiency, and?
questions.explanation: The handbook emphasizes secrecy, efficiency, and loyalty as core values of civil servants Reference: CSH Ethics.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the constitutional body that collects and verifies asset declarations from public officers to enforce ethical standards is the?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) receives asset declarations and monitors compliance with ethical standards Reference: CCB Mandate.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The doctrine of **Separation of Powers** divides governmental authority between which three arms
options: The Public, the Civil Service, and the Military.
The Judiciary, the Executive, and the Legislature.
The Minister, the Permanent Secretary, and the Director.
questions.explanation: The doctrine of separation of powers divides authority between the Judiciary, Executive, and Legislature Reference: Constitutional Knowledge.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **oath of allegiance/oath of office** signed by an officer commits them to uphold and serve faithfully according to the?
questions.explanation: The Oath of Allegiance/Oath of Office is a binding promise to uphold the Constitution and serve faithfully Reference: Constitution/Ethics.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which body ensures that recruitment and disciplinary standards are maintained across the Federal Civil Service?
questions.explanation: The FCSC manages appointments and discipline within the service Reference: FCSC Role.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The **PSR** 2021 mandates that matters not covered in its provisions should be referred to whom for appropriate direction
questions.explanation: Matters not covered in the PSR should be referred to the Head of Service for appropriate direction (Rule 16.10).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who must approve the re-engagement of retired officers into career posts, whose remuneration is based on negotiable terms?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: Re-engagement typically requires approval by the FCSC for career posts (Rule 020412).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which principle guarantees that civil servants serve all citizens and political parties without personal or political preference?
questions.explanation: Impartiality guarantees that civil servants serve all citizens fairly without political or personal bias Reference: CSH Impartiality.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the civil service hierarchy structures staff into categories based on professional disciplines and levels of responsibility, known as?
questions.explanation: The civil service is structured into Grades and Cadres Reference: PSR Structure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the official document used to publish laws, appointments, and constitutional changes in nigeria is the?
questions.explanation: The Official Gazette is the formal government publication used to publish laws, appointments, and constitutional changes Reference: Official Gazette.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: In the case of conflict between the English version and a local language version of the **PSR**, which version prevails
options: The version preferred by the OHCSF.
The version with the earliest commencement date.
questions.explanation: The English version of the PSR prevails in case of conflict with local language versions (Rule 16.19).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the core constitutional and legal document that outlines civil liberties and protections for citizens, including the right to fair hearing, is?
questions.explanation: Chapter IV of the Constitution outlines civil liberties and protections, known as fundamental rights.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: For effective constitutional structure, bodies & principles, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure constitutional structure, bodies & principles compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in constitutional structure, bodies & principles Reference: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set.

questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk management in constitutional-governance work?
questions.explanation: Risk management improves when material exceptions are identified early, escalated promptly, and tracked for follow-up.

questions.explanation: Constitutional work remains defensible when action stays within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards even under pressure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which approach most strongly supports accountable implementation in constitutional structure, bodies, and principles work?
options: Ensure actions remain within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards.
Delay escalation until issues become material and difficult to reverse.
Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.
Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
questions.explanation: Accountable implementation requires action within statutory authority, constitutional safeguards, and the recorded basis for the decision.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When a supervisor reviews gaps in constitutional-governance work, which option best strengthens control and consistency?
options: Action kept within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards.
questions.explanation: Constitutional work remains defensible when action stays within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards even under pressure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which step most directly improves traceability and fairness in constitutional structure, bodies, and principles decisions?
options: Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.
Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
Ensure actions remain within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards.
Rely on verbal approval and close the file without documentary trail.
questions.explanation: Traceability and fairness improve when decisions are tied to authority, records, and constitutional safeguards rather than verbal shortcuts.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What mainly distinguishes the Civil Service from elected officials such as members of the National Assembly?
options: Civil servants do not earn salaries.
Civil servants serve only short fixed terms.
The service has no accumulated institutional experience.
questions.explanation: The Civil Service is a permanent institution with continuity of existence, unlike elected offices that depend on electoral tenure.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Choose the option that NOT one of the fundamental and universal principles for the provision of public services listed in the chapter?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Principle of nepotism.). The principles listed include equality of treatment, neutrality, legality, continuity, proximity and accessibility of services, participation, quality, evaluation, transparency, speed and responsiveness, accountability, motivation, and integrity. Nepotism is a prohibited act under the Principle of Integrity.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What does the principle of neutrality aim to achieve in the Civil Service?
options: To allow civil servants to participate in partisan politics.
To guarantee that civil servants serve the government of the day without political bias.
To prevent civil servants from having any opinions.
To allow civil servants to publicly criticize government policies, a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To guarantee that civil servants serve the government of the day without political bias.). The principle of neutrality secure that civil servants do not take part in partisan politics, serving the government of the day without political bias.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What was the primary focus of the Udoji Public Service Review Commission of 1972?
options: Limiting the powers of the Civil Service.
Increasing efficiency and effectiveness in the Public Service.
Reducing the size of the Civil Service.
questions.explanation: The Udoji Commission is best known for focusing on how to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the Public Service.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What does the principle of neutrality require of Civil Servants?
options: To take part in partisan politics.
To avoid having personal opinions altogether.
To serve the government of the day without political bias.
To criticize government policies publicly as part of their duties.
questions.explanation: The principle of neutrality requires Civil Servants to serve the government of the day faithfully without partisan political bias.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What does unprofessional behaviour imply in a Civil Servant?
options: Treating members of the public with respect.
Displaying rudeness, discourtesy, insensitivity, and inefficiency.
questions.explanation: Unprofessional behaviour in the Civil Service is associated with rudeness, discourtesy, insensitivity, and inefficiency, all of which weaken public confidence.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The principle of neutrality means the public service remains neutral with respect to whom?
questions.explanation: The principle of neutrality requires the public service to serve the government of the day professionally without becoming partisan.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Under the principle of quality, effectiveness, and efficiency, the public service should make optimal use of what?
questions.explanation: This principle requires the public service to deliver high-quality results by making the best possible use of the resources available to it.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the main purpose of records in the Civil Service" according to official standards?
options: To generate a paper trail for all financial transactions.3.
To provide a basis for continuity and reference to the decisions of past governments.
To document the personal lives of civil servants.
To create a historical archive of government actions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To provide a basis for continuity and reference to the decisions of past governments.). Records are kept to provide continuity through reference to the decisions of past governments. The Civil Service's continued relevance is a duty of how well it manages these records.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

To create a historical archive of government actions.
To generate a paper trail for all financial transactions. □. 467
To provide a basis for continuity and reference to the decisions of past governments.

questions.question: What oath do civil servants take on entry into service?
questions.explanation: All civil servants are required to take an Oath of Secrecy. The item therefore tests the formal undertaking tied to responsible handling of official information in public service.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which oath are civil servants required to take?
questions.explanation: Civil servants are required to take an Oath of Secrecy. The oath underlines the duty to protect official information and handle government records responsibly.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What type of safe is required for the daily working balance of cash, stamps, and receipt or licence books during office hours?
options: A safe built into, or securely attached to, the structure of the building.
questions.explanation: The required safe is one built into, or securely attached to, the structure of the building so the daily working balance is kept securely.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which report must be obtained for accidents involving government vehicles under FR 2014?
questions.explanation: Police reports provide the formal incident record required for accidents involving government vehicles.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What conduct is treated as an act of disloyalty for a civil servant?
options: Refusing to carry out an unlawful order.
Giving constructive criticism to a superior in private.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Publicly criticizing the government.). Public criticism of government policies and acts is an act of disloyalty on the part of a Civil Servant.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the objective of the Charter for the Public Service in Africa" according to official standards?
options: To create a new political structure for African nations.
To standardize salaries for civil servants across Africa.
To promote a single currency for all African countries.3.
To streamline services provided to citizens and guarantee the efficient use of resources.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To streamline services provided to citizens and guarantee the efficient use of resources.). The Charter expresses the will and commitment of African States to streamline 439 the services provided to citizens and the desire to secure the efficient use of the resources allocated for such services.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
keywords: charter for the public service in africa
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the official rule for MDAs embarking on foreign trips" according to official standards?
options: They only need to inform the Head of the Civil Service.
They should not embark on such trips without informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
They may travel independently if self-funded.
They must obtain Presidency approval in every case.
questions.explanation: MDAs and related bodies are expected to inform the Ministry of Foreign Affairs before embarking on foreign trips so the official protocol is respected.
questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.chapter: Constitutional Structure, Bodies & Principles - Expansion Set

options: They must serve the government of the day without political bias.
They should publicly criticize government policies.
They must not have personal opinions.
They should take part in partisan politics.
questions.explanation: The principle of neutrality requires civil servants to serve the government of the day faithfully, without partisan bias and without taking part in partisan politics.

questions.question: What oath formalizes a civil servant's duty of confidentiality?
questions.explanation: A civil servant formalizes the duty of confidentiality by taking the Oath of Secrecy. The item therefore tests the oath linked to safeguarding official information.

questions.question: How should the daily working balance of cash, stamps, and receipt or licence books be secured during office hours?
options: In a safe built into, or securely attached to, the building structure.
In any safe available in the office.
questions.explanation: Daily working balances should be kept in a safe that is built into, or firmly attached to, the structure of the building so custody remains secure.

subcategories.name: Legal Frameworks & Statutory Compliance
subcategories.description: Applicable laws and compliance obligations in public service.

questions.question: Which legal framework provides the guidelines for prudence, transparency, and accountability in the management of public finance in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: The Fiscal Responsibility Act provides the legal framework for prudent management of public finances in Nigeria.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 58** of the public procurement act (**ppa**) prescribes penalties for offenses such as bid rigging, collusion, and procurement fraud, including dismissal or?
options: Payment of a minor fine to the MDA.
questions.explanation: Section 58 prescribes imprisonment and fines for procurement-related fraud. Officers involved also face dismissal as serious misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the legal principle that prohibits civil servants from disclosing sensitive government information without authorization is enforced by the?
questions.explanation: The Official Secrets Act enforces secrecy requirements for civil servants handling official matters.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which body is empowered to blacklist or debar contracting firms found guilty of corrupt practices or fraudulent misrepresentation during a bidding process?
questions.explanation: Section 57 empowers the BPP to blacklist bidders for engaging in corrupt practices.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the in a disciplinary inquiry, documentary evidence cannot be used against the officer unless he is given open access thereto, ensuring the principle of?
questions.explanation: No documentary evidence shall be used against the officer unless he has previously been supplied with a copy or given open access thereto (Rule 100307(vii)) [36-38].
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the mandatory pre-qualification requirement for bidders, ensuring they have fulfilled tax obligations to the federal government, is the submission of a valid?
questions.explanation: Section 16 of the PPA requires contractors to demonstrate eligibility, including fulfilling tax obligations.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the penalty of **surcharge** is imposed on an officer to recover?
options: Administrative processing costs charged during disciplinary proceedings.
The exact amount of financial loss caused by the officer's negligence or fault.
Future salary increments due to the officer in subsequent years.
Legal defense expenses incurred by the Ministry in court.
questions.explanation: Surcharge is the recovery of government losses from the officer's pay, applied when the loss is due to the officer’s fault or negligence Reference: FR Sanctions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which legal principle in procurement mandates strict adherence to legal and procedural requirements in awarding contracts to guarantee transparency?
questions.explanation: Due Process mandates strict adherence to legal and procedural requirements in awarding contracts [41-43] Reference: PPA Due Process.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the use of e-procurement systems (**ppa** **section 38**) in public procurement is primarily aimed at enhancing?
questions.explanation: E-procurement enhances efficiency and transparency Reference: PPA Section 38.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: An officer must promptly report a criminal charge or the outcome of such charge to whom, under pain of disciplinary action
options: His Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: An Officer must promptly report a criminal charge or the outcome of such charge to his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office (Rule 100409).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which specific **PPA** Section prohibits procurement without approved budgetary provision and availability of funds?
questions.explanation: Section 14 prohibits procurement without approved budgetary provision and funds in place.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the practice of illegally breaking down a contract into smaller units to circumvent approval limits is explicitly prohibited in the **ppa** as?
questions.explanation: Contract Splitting is explicitly prohibited (PPA Section 58(4)) as it undermines transparency and circumvents established thresholds.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the minimum mandatory period for the archive retention of all procurement records and documents by **mdas** is?
questions.explanation: Procurement documents must be retained for at least 5 years to support potential audits and inquiries (PPA Section 38).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the statutory default and preferred method for the procurement of goods, works, and services that promotes transparency and fair competition is?
questions.explanation: Open Competitive Bidding is the default procurement method under Section 24 of the PPA 2007.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who must authorize emergency procurements, subject to later audit and ratification, as required under **PPA** **Section 42**'?
options: The Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Minister authorizes emergency procurements, often in line with procurement laws and thresholds, subject to later review Reference: PPA Section 42.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If additional grounds for dismissal are disclosed during a disciplinary inquiry, what must the **FCSC** furnish the officer with?
options: A written termination letter effective immediately without hearing.
A written statement of the new grounds, with due-process steps repeated as for the original grounds.
A recommendation for compulsory retirement pending pension approval.
A transfer directive to another department pending final decision.
questions.explanation: If further grounds are disclosed during the inquiry, the officer must be furnished with a written statement of the new grounds and the same due-process steps must be repeated as for the original grounds under Rule 100307(viii).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the the rule that the head of the officer’s department shall not be a member of the board of inquiry tasked with investigating the officer (**rule 100307(v**)) safeguards?
questions.explanation: The head of the Officer's department shall not be a member of the board (Rule 100307(v)). This prevents bias and ensures a fair hearing.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: To preserve transparency in public procurement, how should bid opening be conducted?
options: Opening restricted to the Ministerial Tenders Board only.
Secret opening to protect quoted prices.
Public opening with bidders or their representatives invited.
Opening deferred until after contract award.
questions.explanation: Bid opening should be conducted publicly, with bidders or their representatives invited, so the process can be observed and recorded transparently.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the final check that confirms goods or services were delivered satisfactorily before any payment is processed is documented by the?
questions.explanation: No payment is processed without the Job Completion Certificate (JCC), which confirms that work was done or goods were received Reference: FR Payment Validation.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which **PPA** Section requires bid opening to be conducted in public?
questions.explanation: Section 27 requires that bid opening must be done publicly to ensure fairness and transparency.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the mandatory requirement that bidders must not be in receivership, bankruptcy, or insolvency is contained in **ppa**?
questions.explanation: Section 16 states that eligible contractors must not be in receivership, bankruptcy, or insolvency.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the maximum penalty for an officer convicted of a felony (criminal offense) in addition to possible automatic dismissal?
options: Reduction in salary for six months.
questions.explanation: Serious offenses, especially fraud/corruption (PPA Section 58), lead to dismissal and criminal charges/imprisonment.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the officer who must sign all **payment vouchers** before payments are processed, confirming the correctness and classification of the transaction, is the?
questions.explanation: The Head of Accounts must sign all payment vouchers before payments are processed Reference: FR Payment Approval.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the primary role of the internal auditor is compromised if they report directly to the minister instead of the accounting officer (permanent secretary), due to lack of?
questions.explanation: Internal Audit functions must maintain independence and objectivity; reporting to the Accounting Officer ensures accountability within the MDA structure, while ministerial reporting risks political interference Reference: FR Internal Audit.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section of the PPA allows framework agreements for repeated procurements under pre-agreed terms?
questions.explanation: Section 35 provides for framework agreements that allow repeated procurements under pre-agreed terms.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which principle in the Civil Service requires public officers to serve without seeking personal recognition for policies implemented by the Government?
questions.explanation: Anonymity means civil servants serve the government and maintain policy continuity without seeking personal recognition Reference: CSH Principles.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an officer is required to present himself for examination by a medical board and fails to comply with the instructions, they are liable to be treated as having been?
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails to comply with this rule shall render himself liable to be treated as having been absent without leave (Rule 130307).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the in a disciplinary investigation involving serious misconduct, the board of inquiry shall consist of not less than?
questions.explanation: The Commission may set up a board of inquiry which shall consist of not less than three persons (Rule 100307(v)).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **ppa** principle that requires all bidders be treated fairly and equitably is emphasized in?
questions.explanation: Section 16 requires equal and fair treatment of all bidders during procurement.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 32** of the **ppa** mandates transparency in contract awards by requiring?
options: Publication of Ministerial Tenders Board Minutes only.
Public access to procurement records and contract awards above thresholds.
BPP approval for all low-value contracts.
questions.explanation: Section 32 requires publication of contract awards above thresholds and provides for public access to procurement records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: An officer involved in procurement fraud, such as collusion or over-invoicing, faces which penalty under the **FR** and **PPA** framework
questions.explanation: Officers involved in procurement abuse and fraud face dismissal or criminal charges, as this constitutes serious misconduct (PPA Section 58 / FR Sanctions).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the maximum acting appointment period shall not exceed one year, but in exceptional circumstances may be extended for a further period of?
questions.explanation: The acting appointment shall not exceed one year, but may be extended for a further period of six months (Rule 020703).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT required to be notified simultaneously of decisions regarding the deferment or withholding of increment (**Rule 040209**)?
options: The Office of the Accountant General of the Federation.
The Office of the Auditor General for the Federation.
questions.explanation: Decisions must be communicated to the AGF, AuGF, and the OHCSF [72-74] Reference: Rule 040209.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What happens when an officer on training abroad receives salary from an overseas employer during the training period?
options: It forfeits Nigerian emoluments without OHCSF approval.
questions.explanation: Receiving salary from an overseas employer during the training period affects entitlement to Nigerian emoluments unless the required approval has been obtained.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the separation of roles—authorization, custody, and recording—in financial transactions is the first control measure against fraud, known as?
questions.explanation: The separation of roles (Segregation of Duties) is the first line of defense against financial fraud [78-80] Reference: FR Internal Controls.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which approach best supports effective legal and statutory compliance?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Effective legal and statutory compliance depends on the consistent use of lawful criteria together with transparent documentation of each decision.
questions.chapter: Legal Frameworks & Statutory Compliance - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best strengthens risk management in legal and statutory compliance work?
options: Early escalation of control gaps and material exceptions.
questions.explanation: Risk management in legal and statutory compliance work improves when control gaps and material exceptions are identified early and escalated promptly.
questions.chapter: Legal Frameworks & Statutory Compliance - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When handling legal frameworks & statutory compliance, which choice reflects proper constitutional supremacy standards?
options: Ensure administrative actions remain within constitutional limits.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.

Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.

questions.question: Which approach best supports rights balancing in legal and statutory compliance work?
options: Narrow exemptions with recorded legal justification.
questions.explanation: Rights balancing in legal and statutory compliance work is strongest when exemptions are applied narrowly and the legal basis for each decision is recorded clearly.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on public law standards within Legal Frameworks & Statutory Compliance?
options: Maintain fairness, reasonableness, and procedural propriety.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.

questions.question: Which practice best supports documented procedure in legal and statutory compliance work?
options: Complete records under the approved procedure.
questions.explanation: Documented procedure in legal and statutory compliance work depends on following the approved process and keeping complete records of the steps taken.

questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Legal Frameworks & Statutory Compliance?
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.

Depositing the funds in the bank. Q

questions.question: Which system automates payroll, eliminates ghost workers, and tracks civil servant identity records?

questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The Treasury Single Account (**TSA**) system was introduced primarily to achieve which of the following financial objectives
options: Allow MDAs to manage separate bank accounts.
Consolidate all government revenue into a single account to improve transparency.
questions.explanation: TSA consolidates government revenues into a single account managed by the CBN to improve transparency and block leakages Reference: FR Reforms – TSA.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the primary the primary objective of staff development and training (**rule 070101**) is to enhance the knowledge, skills, attitude, effectiveness, and?
questions.explanation: Staff Development aims to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of staff in order to meet their career goals and prepare them for changing duties and responsibilities (Rule 070101).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the consequence for an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts (**Rule 030104**)?
options: Reduction in rank for one year.
Required to resign or be withdrawn from the service.
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to resign or be withdrawn from the service Reference: Rule 030104.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the compulsory retirement age for all civil servants in the federal public service is?
options: 65 years of age or 30 years of service, whichever comes first.
60 years or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever comes first.
55 years of age or 35 years of service, whichever comes first.
60 years of age regardless of pensionable service records.
questions.explanation: The compulsory retirement age is 60 years or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever comes first (Rule 020909).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the main the primary purpose of **record-keeping** in the civil service handbook is emphasized to ensure?
options: Accountability and continuity of government operations.
questions.explanation: Proper record-keeping ensures accountability, transparency, and continuity in civil service operations Reference: CSH Records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the the responsibility for issuing establishment circulars that amend, clarify, or interpret the public service rules (**psr**) rests with the?
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: Circulars issued by the Head of Service or Presidency update or supplement the rules and provide interim directives Reference: PSR/OHCSF Role.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which administrative body is responsible for handling public complaints against administrative injustice in the public service?
questions.explanation: The Public Complaints Commission addresses grievances and promotes administrative justice Reference: General Knowledge.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of discouraging **nepotism** in the Civil Service Handbook?

questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What does the principle of transparency require in public decision-making?
options: Restricted circulation of all official information.
Open decisions and processes with records available for accountability.
Budget details known only to senior officers.
questions.explanation: Transparency requires decisions and processes to be open enough for review, with records available so accountability can be tested later.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer must discharge every duty assigned to him by government and accept liability of being stationed wherever his presence is considered to be most useful, as per?

questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who is statutorily designated as the Chief Financial Adviser to the Federal Government?
questions.explanation: The Minister of Finance is the Chief Financial Adviser to the Federal Government Reference: FR General Management.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the period of service rendered during **study leave without pay** shall be regarded as?
questions.explanation: The period of study shall not be regarded as a break in Service (Rule 120230(b)).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the mandatory pre-retirement leave for officers who give notice of retirement is?
questions.explanation: The pre-retirement leave is three months before retirement (Rule 120243).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The Accountant-General of the Federation is responsible for issuing which key financial instrument that provides detailed instructions on financial procedures and implementation
questions.explanation: The Accountant-General of the Federation issues Treasury Circulars Reference: FR General Management.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the in the civil service, **seniority** is primarily determined by?
options: Grade level and date of promotion/appointment.
questions.explanation: Seniority within a grade level is determined by the date of promotion to that grade Reference: PSR Seniority.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the main the purpose of the **contingencies fund** is to cover?
questions.explanation: The Contingencies Fund is used to meet unforeseen and urgent government expenditures [118-120] Reference: FR General Management.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the statutory standard incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted to a post in the federal public service shall be?
options: The date of appointment or promotion irrespective of service duration.
The 1st January of the year following at least six months of service or promotion.
The end of the current quarter after assumption of duty.
The 31st December of the same year as appointment or promotion.
questions.explanation: The incremental date is the 1st January of the year following at least six months of service or promotion (Rule 040203).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which body coordinates and monitors the implementation of the Performance Management System (PMS) across **MDAs** to ensure national uniformity?
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
questions.explanation: The OHCSF coordinates and monitors the implementation of PMS across MDAs Reference: OHCSF Role – PMS.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the goal of promoting **respect for diversity** in the civil service handbook is primarily aimed at achieving?
options: Inclusivity and unity in the workplace.
questions.explanation: Respecting diversity fosters inclusivity, unity, and fairness in the workplace Reference: CSH Diversity.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Under Rule 040204, what makes withholding an increment more serious than deferring it?
options: Permanent loss that cannot be restored retrospectively.
Automatic promotion after the punishment period.
questions.explanation: Withholding an increment is more serious because the loss cannot be restored retrospectively, whereas deferral only postpones the increment.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the maximum duration of **study leave with pay** that may be granted to an officer is?
questions.explanation: The duration of study leave with pay shall not exceed two years (Rule 120228(a)).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the system used to manage digital hr records, including staff leave, postings, and promotions, is known as?
questions.explanation: The Human Resource Management Information System (HRMIS) enables digital management of staff records, leave, postings, and promotions Reference: ICT Reforms.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Under the PSR rule on working hours, what is excluded when calculating official working hours?
questions.explanation: The Public Service Rules definition of working hours excludes all officially recognized break periods. The item therefore tests what is left out when official working hours are being calculated under the rule.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which outcome best reflects efficiency in public service delivery?
options: Services that meet public needs and deliver value for money.
Politically neutral wording in every decision.
questions.explanation: Efficiency in service delivery is shown when services meet public needs effectively and deliver value for the resources used.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the mandatory requirement for promotion eligibility, in addition to merit and seniority, is that the officer must have undergone?

Structured training and acquired skill competence per scheme of service.
questions.explanation: The officer must have undergone structured training and acquired skill competence as specified in the scheme of service (Rule 020803(a)).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

60 years or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever comes first.

Allowing employees to make their own rules.
Providing employees with a pleasant and favourable working environment.

subcategories.name: Access Rights & Obligations (FOI)
subcategories.description: Rights to access public records and institutional obligations.

questions.question: In which year was the Freedom of Information Act enacted in Nigeria
questions.explanation: The Freedom of Information Act was signed into law in 2011, so 2011 is the correct enactment year.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Freedom of Information Act?
options: To restrict information flow to foreign entities.
To ensure public access to information held by public institutions.
To empower only the judiciary in document review.
To limit freedom of the press.
questions.explanation: The FOI Act aims to make public records accessible to citizens, enhancing transparency and accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Under the **FOI** Act, within how many days must a public institution generally respond to a request for information
questions.explanation: Section 4 of the FOI Act stipulates that public institutions must respond to requests within 7 days.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section of the **FOI** Act guarantees the right of any person to access public records?
questions.explanation: Section 1 of the FOI Act guarantees the right of any person to access or request information from public institutions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the right of access under the **foi** act extends to?
options: Nigerian citizens and registered NGOs only.
questions.explanation: Section 1 makes the right of access available to 'any person,' not limited to Nigerian citizens.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the maximum extension period allowed for an **FOI** request if the initial 7-day period is insufficient?
questions.explanation: The Act allows a maximum extension of 7 additional days to respond where necessary.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section of the **FOI** Act requires that information be provided in a language understandable to the applicant, when reasonably practicable?
questions.explanation: Section 3(4) requires that information be provided in a language understandable to the applicant when reasonably practicable.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If a public institution does not hold the requested information but knows another institution does, which section requires the institution to transfer the request
questions.explanation: Section 7 provides for transfer of requests where the information is held by another institution.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section provides for public institutions to charge fees to cover the cost of duplication and transcription of records?
questions.explanation: Section 8(1) allows institutions to charge fees covering costs of duplication or transcription of records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is explicitly stated by **Section 1**(2) regarding the applicant's motivation for requesting information?
options: The applicant must demonstrate financial interest.
The applicant must provide strong reasons for the request.
No applicant needs to demonstrate interest or reasons.
The request must be legally sanctioned.
questions.explanation: Section 1(2) makes clear that no applicant needs to demonstrate interest or reasons for requesting information.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section of the **FOI** Act mandates every public institution to maintain proper records for easy access?
questions.explanation: Section 2 of the FOI Act mandates public institutions to maintain proper records for easy access.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section of the **FOI** Act obliges institutions to proactively publish certain categories of information without waiting for requests?
questions.explanation: Section 2 requires public institutions to proactively publish categories of information without waiting for requests.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the act requires institutions to train staff on records management and access procedures to ensure?
questions.explanation: The Act requires institutions to train staff to ensure effective implementation and compliance.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section mandates public institutions to submit annual compliance reports on **FOI** requests to the Attorney-General?
questions.explanation: Section 2(7) mandates public institutions to submit annual compliance reports on FOI requests to the Attorney-General of the Federation.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section requires institutions to designate **FOI** desk officers responsible for handling requests?
questions.explanation: Section 5(1) requires every institution to designate officers responsible for handling FOI requests.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: **Section 6**(2) obliges institutions to maintain records in which format for easy access
questions.explanation: Section 6(2) requires public institutions to keep records in formats that make them easily accessible, such as machine-readable versions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 2**(3) mandates public institutions to widely disseminate information through various media to the public. this relates to?
questions.explanation: Section 2(3) mandates wide dissemination of information, aligning with the principle of proactive disclosure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section requires public institutions to maintain updated indexes of their records for accessibility?
questions.explanation: Section 6(1) mandates public institutions to maintain updated indexes of their records for accessibility.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: **Section 2**(3) emphasizes the proactive disclosure of which categories of records, even without a formal request
questions.explanation: Section 2(3) emphasizes proactive disclosure of categories like budgets, contracts, and financial data.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If an applicant is denied access, which recourse is available to them under the Act (**Section 20**)
options: Appeal immediately to the Permanent Secretary.
File an action in a court of law.
Appeal to the Head of Service.
questions.explanation: Section 20 provides that applicants may challenge denials of access in a court of law.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who is responsible for oversight and submitting an annual report on **FOI** compliance to the National Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Attorney-General of the Federation is responsible for oversight, issuing guidelines, and submitting an annual report to the National Assembly (Section 32).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which legal entity bears the burden of proof when a request for information is denied?
questions.explanation: Section 24 places the burden of proof on the institution that denies access to information.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is required of public institutions regarding compliance reports under **Section 2**(7)?
options: Daily updates to the Minister of Finance.
Annual submission to the Attorney-General of the Federation.
Quarterly reports to the National Assembly.
Verbal confirmation to the Head of Service.
questions.explanation: Section 2(7) mandates annual compliance reports to the Attorney-General of the Federation.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the fee chargeable under **section 8**(1) for fulfilling an **foi** request is limited to covering the cost of?
questions.explanation: Section 8(1) allows fees covering costs of duplication or transcription of records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: **Section 5**(1) addresses which core institutional capacity requirement for **FOI** implementation
questions.explanation: Section 5(1) mandates the designation of officers responsible for handling FOI requests.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an **mda** denies an **foi** request, the denial must be accompanied by?
options: A cheque covering the transcription fee.
The specific section of the Act justifying the exemption.
questions.explanation: A denial must clearly state the statutory ground for exemption and the section invoked, aligning with procedural compliance (General FOI practice, not explicitly cited in all steps, but derived from the principle of judicial appeal).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **foi** act requires institutions to maintain updated **indexes** of their records primarily to facilitate?
options: Public identification and request of existing records.
questions.explanation: Section 6(1) mandates indexes for public accessibility and identification of records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of these categories is emphasized for **proactive disclosure** under **Section 2**(3)?
options: Records of all contracts and expenditure.
questions.explanation: Section 2(3) emphasizes proactive disclosure of categories like budgets, contracts, and financial data.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the response time for an **foi** request (7 days) is crucial because failure to respond within this period is legally deemed as?
options: A waiver of the duplication fee.
questions.explanation: Failure to respond within the statutory time frame is considered a deemed refusal, allowing the applicant recourse to court (Implied by Section 4 and Section 20).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which official has the authority to issue guidelines for the implementation and compliance of the **FOI** Act?
questions.explanation: The Attorney-General of the Federation issues implementation guidelines and monitors compliance Reference: Oversight.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if a public institution fails to submit its annual compliance report to the attorney-general, it is in breach of?
questions.explanation: Section 2(7) requires annual compliance reports, so failure to submit the report breaches that provision.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The **FOI** Act is said to complement which constitutional right of Nigerian citizens
options: Right to fair hearing under procedural justice provisions.
Right to life under constitutional human rights guarantees.
Freedom of expression and information, in line with formal public service practice.
Right to peaceful assembly and association for citizens.
questions.explanation: The FOI Act operationalizes the constitutional right to freedom of expression and information (Section 39).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the for efficiency, institutions are required to keep records in formats that make them easily accessible, as per **section 6**(2). this is vital for promoting?
questions.explanation: Keeping records in easily accessible (machine-readable) formats supports digital governance and efficient service delivery Reference: E-Governance Records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under **section 7**, if an **mda** receives a request for information that primarily concerns another **mda**, the receiving **mda** must transfer the request within?
questions.explanation: The FOI Act requires requests to be transferred expeditiously, usually within 3 working days (Derived from Section 7 and general FOI implementation guidelines).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When may an applicant waive the right to challenge a denial of access in court?
questions.explanation: A denial of access does not itself amount to a waiver of the applicant's right to challenge the denial in court.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the proactive disclosure duty under **section 2** mandates that public institutions publish information that is relevant to?
options: The functioning of the institution and the public's access rights.
questions.explanation: Proactive disclosure covers information regarding the institution's function, structure, decisions, and financial dealings Reference: Proactive Disclosure Scope.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the to ensure effective compliance, **section 5** requires public institutions to maintain?
options: Regular staff training programs for officials.
questions.explanation: Section 5 requires public institutions to train officials to ensure effective implementation of the Act.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which document is the Attorney-General of the Federation mandated to submit annually to the National Assembly regarding **FOI**'?
options: A report on FOI implementation and compliance.
The list of all staff promotions.
questions.explanation: Section 32 requires the Attorney-General to submit an annual report on FOI implementation to the National Assembly.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the right to access information under the **foi** act is limited by?
options: The Permanent Secretary's personal discretion in each request.
The applicant's ability to prove political neutrality beforehand.
Specific statutory exemptions defined in the Act (Sections 11-19).
The amount of duplication and transmission fees paid.
questions.explanation: The right to access is restricted only by the specific exemptions defined in the Act, such as those related to national security or privacy [29-31].
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **foi** act applies to?
options: All public institutions under the Federal Government.
Only private companies handling government contracts.
questions.explanation: The FOI Act applies broadly to all public institutions under the Federal Government, including MDAs.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What must a state government do if it wants the FOI Act to apply to its institutions?
options: Treat the Act as automatically applicable at once.
Domesticate the Act through its House of Assembly.
Wait for extension by federal circular.
Rely on the Federal Character Principle.
questions.explanation: A state that wants the FOI regime to apply to its own institutions must domesticate the Act through its legislature.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What does proactive disclosure under section 2 of the FOI Act require?
options: Publication only after a court order.
Publication of specified information without waiting for a request.
Publication only on the anniversary of the Act.
questions.explanation: Proactive disclosure requires public institutions to publish specified information without waiting for a formal request.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 3**(4) requires that the form in which access is granted should be preferred by the applicant, unless?
options: The applicant is a foreign national.
It is impracticable to produce in that form.
questions.explanation: Institutions must comply with the applicant's preferred format unless it is impracticable or infringes on copyrights (General FOI principle, aligned with accessible formats Section 6(2)).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If an officer discloses information in good faith, believing that the disclosure is required under the **FOI** Act, which section protects that officer from civil or criminal liability?
questions.explanation: Section 27 of the FOI Act protects an officer who discloses information in good faith from civil or criminal liability.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the establishment of **foi** desk officers (**section 5**(1)) is a strategy to ensure?
options: Streamlined processing and prompt response to requests.
questions.explanation: Designated officers ensure clear responsibility, leading to streamlined processing and prompt response Reference: Implementation Strategy.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What follows if a public institution exceeds the statutory response time without notifying the applicant of an extension?
questions.explanation: If the statutory response time passes without a valid extension notice, the request is treated as denied and the applicant may go to court.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section requires public institutions to keep training programs for their officials to maintain adequate records and address access procedures?
questions.explanation: Section 5 requires public institutions to train officials, so that section matches the question on staff training.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the requirement in **section 6**(1) for maintaining updated indexes of records enhances?
options: Institutional secrecy over records management and disclosure.
The public's ability to identify relevant records before formal request.
Political mobilization and fundraising by civic associations.
Internal disciplinary action for records officers by default.
questions.explanation: Indexes improve accessibility and help the public identify specific records needed Reference: Record Management.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **foi** act fundamentally enhances governance in nigeria by promoting transparency and?
questions.explanation: The FOI Act promotes transparency and accountability in governance, so facilitating accountability is the correct completion.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act
