questions.explanation: Public accountability depends on decisions that can be traced to recorded reasons, supporting facts, and a reviewable legal basis.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: For effective exemptions & public interest (foi), what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk control in FOI exemption work?
options: Untracked exceptions after a control failure.
questions.explanation: Risk control is stronger when identified risks are matched with documented mitigation and follow-up action rather than left to informal discretion.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When handling exemptions & public interest (foi), which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best sustains operational discipline in FOI exemption work?
options: Skipped workflow checks to save time.
Personal preference in routine case handling.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline depends on completing approved workflow checks and verifying outputs before a file is closed or advanced.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: In the context of Exemptions & Public Interest (**FOI**), which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best supports record management in FOI exemption work?
options: Current files with status updates at each control point.
Incomplete file updates after key actions.
questions.explanation: Record management is stronger when files stay current and each control point is reflected in a status update that later reviewers can verify.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Exemptions & Public Interest (**FOI**)?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain exemptions & public interest (foi) compliance in Exemptions & Public Interest (**FOI**)?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: In the context of Exemptions & Public Interest (**FOI**), which action best demonstrates exemptions & public interest (foi) risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: For effective exemptions & public interest (foi), what is the most appropriate approach to ensure constitutional supremacy
options: Ensure administrative actions remain within constitutional limits.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Ensure administrative actions remain within constitutional limits. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best supports legal compliance in FOI exemption work?
options: Legal-authority checks with a documented decision basis.
Action before checking the governing legal power.
Different standards for similar legal questions.
questions.explanation: Legal compliance is more defensible when officers confirm the governing legal authority before acting and record the basis for the decision clearly.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When handling exemptions & public interest (foi), which choice reflects proper foi obligations standards?
options: Respond to information requests within legal timelines.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Respond to information requests within legal timelines. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in exemptions & public interest (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which routine best sustains rights balancing in FOI exemption decisions?
options: Narrow exemptions with a recorded legal basis.
Broad secrecy without a stated legal basis.
questions.explanation: Rights balancing is most defensible when exemptions are applied narrowly and every restriction is tied to a clear legal basis on the record.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When an FOI exemptions unit faces competing priorities, which action best preserves legal defensibility and service quality?
options: Shortcuts taken outside the governing legal framework.
Review checkpoints bypassed because deadlines were tight.
Action kept within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards.
questions.explanation: FOI work remains defensible when action stays within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards even under pressure to act quickly.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: During routine FOI exemptions and public-interest operations, which approach most strongly supports accountable implementation?
options: Ensure actions remain within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards.
Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.
Delay escalation until issues become material and difficult to reverse.
questions.explanation: FOI exemption and public-interest decisions remain accountable when officers stay within statutory authority and constitutional safeguards rather than treating exceptions casually.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Choose the best answer for: What must the salary section be informed of in writing at the earliest possible time?
options: Any variation that may affect the salary of any officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Any variation that may affect the salary of any officer.). Financial Regulation 1522(i) demand that the salary section must be informed in writing of any variation that may affect an officer's salary at the earliest possible time.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When cash has to be transported from one place to another, what must be provided to the accountable officer?
options: A receipt for the cash only.
An approved cash tank or box, a government vehicle, and a police escort.
questions.explanation: When cash is transported from one place to another, the accountable officer must be provided with an approved cash tank or box, a government vehicle, and a police escort, as required by the Financial Regulations.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Choose the best answer for: What information must the Last payment Certificate and Personal Emolument log show?
options: The last disbursement date, details of deductions, and the balance recoverable.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The last disbursement date, details of deductions, and the balance recoverable.). According to Financial Regulation 1514(ii), the last disbursement certificate and personal emolument document must show the last disbursement date, full details of deductions, and the balance recoverable.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: According to the handbook, what is a key asset for a schedule officer?
options: Accurate information based on facts and figures.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Accurate information based on facts and figures.). An important asset to most scheduled officers is accurate information based on 433 facts and figures.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "considered an important asset for a schedule officer" according to official standards?
options: Accurate information based on facts and figures.
questions.explanation: An important asset for a schedule officer is accurate information based on facts and figures, because sound administrative decisions depend on reliable records and evidence.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What should a good brief try to recommend?
options: A solution to an outstanding problem.
questions.explanation: A good brief should help decision-making by recommending a workable solution to the problem under consideration.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What information must be printed on all receipt books?
options: Only the title of the book.
The name of the issuing officer.
The title of the book, the serial number of each receipt, and the number of the book.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1203 requires the title of the book, the serial number of each receipt, and the book number to be printed on all receipt books.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What should an officer do when they receive a circular that does not demand action?
options: Minute the file to others for information and disposal.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Minute the file to others for information and disposal.). If he does not necessitate actually to initiate any action he will either merely minute the file to others for information and disposal or he will file it.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What information should be included in the quarterly returns on government vehicles, in addition to mileage and fuel consumption?
questions.explanation: Quarterly vehicle returns should also capture repair cost so the running cost of the vehicle can be reviewed.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What should a Civil Servant do if a file has remained on the desk for a long time without action?
options: Report it to a superior officer and return it to the registry.
Keep it in the desk until someone asks for it.
Pass it to a colleague without record.
questions.explanation: If a file remains on an officer's desk for too long without action, the matter should be reported to a superior officer and the file returned to the registry for proper control and follow-up.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What should never be used for drafting if it contains classified information?
questions.explanation: Discarded papers containing classified information should never be reused for drafting because they may expose protected information.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What form must be used for an officer's staff log, and how many copies must be sent to the National Log Centre?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Gen 60, one copy). According to Rule 020107(i), a Staff log Form (Gen 60) must be used, and one copy must be sent to the National log Centre.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Choose the best answer for: 163. How many papers must an officer on GL 09 pass to be confirmed?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (All 5 papers of COMPRO I). The syllabus in Rule 030202 lists 5 papers for COMPRO I, which officers on GL 08-09 must pass to be confirmed.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What must be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe for all cash, stamps, receipt/licence books, and other articles deposited?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Registers.). Financial Regulation 1121 demand that "Registers must be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe of all cash, stamps, receipt or licence books and other articles deposited."
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the minimum standard for recording vehicle usage in the logbook" according to official standards?
options: Departure/arrival times, mileage, and objective of journey.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Departure/arrival times, mileage, and objective of journey.). Financial Regulation 2005 details the requirement for recording "full details of every journey performed including mileage, departure and arrival times and destination" in the logbook.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What attitude should a long-serving civil servant avoid after many years in one ministry?
options: Treating personal experience as the only view that matters.
Sharing institutional knowledge with newer officers.
Advising political leadership from an informed professional position.
questions.explanation: Long service should deepen judgment, not harden it into inflexibility. The unhealthy attitude is assuming that personal experience alone settles every issue in the ministry.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What are the normal working hours in Federal offices?
questions.explanation: The normal working hours in Federal offices are 8:00 am to 4:00 pm, Monday to Friday, unless they are officially varied.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

subcategories.name: Offences, Penalties & Enforcement (FOI)
subcategories.description: Sanctions, offences, and enforcement pathways under FOI.

questions.question: What penalty is imposed on any officer who wrongfully denies access to information in violation of the **FOI** Act?
questions.explanation: The FOI Act imposes a fine of ₦500,000 on officers who unlawfully deny access to information.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section of the **FOI** Act criminalizes destruction of public records?
questions.explanation: Section 29 criminalizes destruction or falsification of public records and prescribes penalties.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the fine prescribed for wrongful destruction or falsification of public records under **Section 29**'?
questions.explanation: Section 29 prescribes a ₦500,000 fine for wrongful destruction or falsification of public records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section makes it an offense to alter or falsify public records?
questions.explanation: Section 29 makes alteration or falsification of records a criminal offence with penalties.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 29** prohibits the destruction of records needed for pending **foi** requests. this is intended to ensure?
options: Compliance with the Pension Reform Act.
The integrity and availability of evidence for disclosure.
questions.explanation: Section 29 prohibits destruction of records subject to FOI requests and prescribes penalties.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section criminalizes willful concealment of public records?
questions.explanation: Section 29 criminalizes willful concealment, destruction, or falsification of public records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section provides protection from liability for officers who disclose information in good faith?
questions.explanation: Section 27 provides immunity for officers who release information in good faith.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the fine of ₦500,000 for wrongful denial of access is imposed on?
options: The specific officer responsible for the unlawful denial.
questions.explanation: The fine is imposed on the specific officer who wrongfully denies access Reference: Penalties.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an officer is prosecuted for willful concealment of records (s. 29), this action is typically initiated by?
options: The Federal Civil Service Commission through disciplinary referral.
Law enforcement agencies or the Attorney-General's office.
The Permanent Secretary through internal administrative memo.
The Audit Committee during annual financial review sittings.
questions.explanation: Criminal offences under the FOI Act are subject to prosecution, usually managed by the Attorney-General's office or relevant law enforcement.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: **Section 29** addresses which cluster of criminal activities related to public records
options: Destruction, concealment, or falsification of records.
questions.explanation: Section 29 criminalizes willful concealment, destruction, or falsification of public records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the protection from liability (s. 27) applies when an officer?
options: Releases any document, regardless of secrecy classification.
Releases exempt information despite knowing the harm.
Discloses information in good faith under the Act's mandate.
questions.explanation: Section 27 protects officers only when disclosure is made in good faith.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an officer unlawfully denies an **foi** request and is fined ₦500,000, this penalty is administered through?
questions.explanation: Penalties and enforcement of the FOI Act generally rely on legal action initiated by the applicant (Section 20) or prosecution by the AGF, resulting in court-imposed sanctions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If an institution defends a denial of access in court under Section 20, what must it show in addition to citing an exemption?
options: That the requester obtained prior ministerial approval.
That copying and certification fees were fully paid.
That the public-interest test does not support disclosure.
That a similar record exists somewhere else in government.
questions.explanation: A court challenge requires more than naming an exemption. The institution must justify the refusal and show that disclosure is not required once the public-interest test is considered.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Under Section 29 of the FOI Act, falsification of public records by an officer constitutes what?
options: Criminal offense under the FOI Act.
questions.explanation: Section 29 of the Freedom of Information Act treats falsification of public records by an officer as a criminal offense. The item therefore tests the legal status of that conduct under the Act.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What is the primary the primary objective of imposing penalties for **foi** violations is to?
options: Increase government internally generated revenue collections.
Deter non-compliance and protect the public's right to information.
Fund institutional retreat programs and staff development.
Mandate annual leave compliance for records management staff.
questions.explanation: Penalties are designed to deter unauthorized denial or destruction of records, supporting the Act's transparency objectives.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the protection offered by **section 27** does **not** apply if the officer's disclosure of information was done?
options: Based on the advice of legal counsel.
To comply with a court order.
questions.explanation: The protection relies entirely on the disclosure being made in 'good faith' Reference: Protection (S. 27).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What happens if a public officer is found guilty of an offense under **Section 29**'?
options: Imposition of fine (₦500k) and potential dismissal from service.
A verbal warning and deferred promotion.
Mandatory attendance at a training course.
questions.explanation: Section 29 prescribes penalties including fines (₦500,000). Criminal conviction typically leads to dismissal under PSR guidelines.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the willful concealment of public records is treated as an offense primarily because it?
options: Interferes with the smooth flow of official correspondence.
Frustrates the public's right to information guaranteed by the Act.
questions.explanation: Concealment frustrates the purpose of the FOI Act and the public's right of access.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who is responsible for oversight of **FOI** Act compliance among public institutions?
questions.explanation: The Attorney-General of the Federation oversees FOI compliance among public institutions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: The Attorney-General is mandated to submit the annual **FOI** compliance report to which legislative body
options: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC).
questions.explanation: Section 32 requires the Attorney-General to submit an annual report to the National Assembly.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which act is treated as a criminal offense under Section 29 of the FOI Act?
questions.explanation: Section 29 of the Freedom of Information Act identifies falsification of public records as a criminal offense. The item therefore tests recognition of the conduct specifically penalized by that section.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who illegally denies access to information may be liable to a court-imposed fine of?
questions.explanation: The penalty for wrongful denial of access is a fine of ₦500,000 Reference: Penalties.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the provision for the attorney-general to compile and publish annual **foi** compliance reports is found in?
questions.explanation: Section 32 requires the Attorney-General to compile and publish annual FOI compliance reports.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an officer discloses information in good faith, **section 27** protects them from?
questions.explanation: Section 27 protects officers from liability when they disclose information in good faith.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When an **MDA** defends a denial in court, the burden of proving that the record is truly exempt falls upon?
options: The presiding judge handling the FOI enforcement suit.
The Attorney-General's representative for the federation.
The applicant seeking disclosure from the public institution.
questions.explanation: Section 24 places the burden of proof on the institution that denies access to information.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 29** ensures the accountability of officers concerning public records by criminalizing?
questions.explanation: Section 29 criminalizes actions that undermine record integrity, such as concealment and falsification.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the penalty for unlawful destruction of records (s. 29) is a fine of ₦500,000 and potentially?
questions.explanation: Section 29 prescribes fines and potential imprisonment for criminal offences related to records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which section requires that exemptions must be interpreted narrowly in favour of disclosure?
questions.explanation: Section 28(1) requires exemptions to be read narrowly in favour of disclosure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an **mda** that fails to respond to an **foi** request within the statutory timeframe risks?
options: Loss of the institution's entire annual appropriation.
Being deemed to have denied the request and facing court action.
Automatic suspension of the Permanent Secretary from office.
Mandatory promotion of the responsible FOI desk officer.
questions.explanation: Deemed refusal enables the applicant to appeal immediately to court (Section 20).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 29** applies to destruction of records needed for pending **foi** requests, but does not apply to records destroyed as part of routine, lawful?
questions.explanation: The offence applies only to wrongful/unlawful destruction, not routine records management and retention policies Reference: Offences (S. 29) Limit.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If an applicant is denied access, **Section 20** grants them the right to seek redress in a court of competent jurisdiction within how many days of the denial or deemed denial
questions.explanation: Section 20 grants the right to court action within 30 days (General FOI practice, supported by Section 20 providing for court action).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the public institution's obligation to maintain proper indexes of records (s. 6(1)) serves as a procedural countermeasure against accusations of?
questions.explanation: Proper record indexing prevents claims that the institution is concealing records by claiming they don't exist or cannot be located Reference: Record Integrity.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 29** of the **foi** act is primarily concerned with protecting the integrity of government?
questions.explanation: Section 29 criminalizes actions against public records (destruction, falsification, concealment).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if a permanent secretary willfully withholds non-exempt information, they are liable to a fine of?
questions.explanation: The penalty for wrongful denial of access is a fine of ₦500,000 Reference: Penalties.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the authority to issue guidelines for foi implementation rests with the?
questions.explanation: The FOI Act places the guideline-making authority with the Attorney-General of the Federation.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 27** provides protection from liability for officers who disclose information, provided the disclosure is based on the officer's belief that it is required by the act and is made in?
questions.explanation: The crucial condition is disclosure in 'good faith' Reference: Protection (S. 27).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an **mda** fails to designate an **foi** desk officer as required by **section 5**(1), this constitutes a failure in?
questions.explanation: A failure to designate an FOI desk officer is a failure of administrative implementation of the Act.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Who challenges a denial of access in court?
questions.explanation: Under the FOI Act, the requester whose access was denied is the person with standing to apply to court and challenge that denial.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: What must be included in the annual FOI compliance report submitted by the Attorney-General?
options: The personal wealth of all public officers.
The number of requests received, granted, and denied across MDAs.
The total cost of government procurements.
questions.explanation: The annual FOI compliance report must summarize implementation metrics, including the number of requests received, granted, and denied across public institutions.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: If an officer inadvertently destroys a public record while transferring offices, would they be liable under **Section 29**'
options: Only if the destruction was willful or malicious.
No, if they report it within 24 hours.
Yes, if the record was classified 'Secret'.
questions.explanation: Section 29 criminalizes 'willful' concealment, destruction, or falsification. Inadvertent destruction lacks the requisite intent for this criminal offense.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: When an **MDA** grants access, the cost of copying (duplication/transcription) is recovered under?
questions.explanation: Section 8(1) allows institutions to charge fees covering costs of duplication or transcription.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which **FOI** provision states that exemptions must be narrowly construed?
questions.explanation: Section 28(1) of the FOI Act states that exemptions must be interpreted narrowly in favour of disclosure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an **mda** denies a request, the applicant may appeal to court within 30 days of the denial or?
options: The date the FOI Act was enacted.
The deemed denial date (7 days after request).
The end of the current fiscal year.
The date of the next promotion board meeting.
questions.explanation: Appeal period runs from the denial or the date the institution was deemed to have denied the request Reference: Appeals Time Limit.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 27**, protecting officers who disclose in good faith, incentivizes?
options: Strict adherence to the Official Secrets Act only.
questions.explanation: The protection encourages officers to err on the side of transparency and disclosure, supporting the Act's purpose.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the failure to produce records in accessible, machine-readable formats (s. 6(2)) undermines?
options: The accessibility objective of the Act.
The calculation of the Contingencies Fund.
questions.explanation: Section 6(2) aims to make records easily accessible, especially digitally.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if a public institution fails to keep proper records as mandated by **section 2**, they may be subject to legal action for?
options: Breach of statutory duty under the FOI Act.
Violation of civil service promotion rules.
questions.explanation: Failure to maintain proper records is a breach of statutory duty Reference: Obligations Failure.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which body ensures the enforcement of the criminal offences outlined in **Section 29**'?
options: The Judiciary, supported by the AGF’s office.
questions.explanation: The Judiciary handles criminal prosecution, with oversight and initiation often by the Attorney-General Reference: Enforcement.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer found to have willfully concealed records (s. 29) is liable to pay?
questions.explanation: Section 29 prescribes a fine of N500,000 for willful concealment of records.
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which of the following describes the **section 28** promotes accountability by ensuring the judiciary can interpret exemptions narrowly, thereby minimizing?
options: The abuse of exemptions to cover up illegal activities.
questions.explanation: Narrow interpretation and the public interest test minimize the use of exemptions to shield illegal activity from public view Reference: Public Interest (S. 28).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: For effective offences, penalties & enforcement (foi), what is the most appropriate approach to ensure offences, penalties & enforcement (foi) compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in offences, penalties & enforcement (foi).
questions.sourceDocument: Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and FOI Act

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk management in FOI offences, penalties, and enforcement work?

Unreported exceptions left in the file.
